prompt:
The nursery tag-printer package stopped compiling after the v2 device branch merged and I've been handed the cleanup. Compiler says:

  # go-planttags [go-planttags.test]
  ./print.go:27:25: cannot use TextWriter{} (value of struct type TextWriter) as TagWriter value in array or slice literal: TextWriter does not implement TagWriter (missing method WriteTag)
  ./tags.go:20:12: cannot use TextWriter{} (value of struct type TextWriter) as TagWriter value in map literal: TextWriter does not implement TagWriter (missing method WriteTag)
  ./tags.go:21:12: cannot use CSVWriter{…} (value of struct type CSVWriter) as TagWriter value in map literal: CSVWriter does not implement TagWriter (missing method WriteTag)
  ./tags.go:22:12: cannot use BadgeWriter{} (value of struct type BadgeWriter) as TagWriter value in map literal: BadgeWriter does not implement TagWriter (method WriteTag has pointer receiver)
  ./planttags_test.go:23:20: cannot use TextWriter{} (value of struct type TextWriter) as TagWriter value in variable declaration: TextWriter does not implement TagWriter (missing method WriteTag)
  ./planttags_test.go:38:20: cannot use CSVWriter{…} (value of struct type CSVWriter) as TagWriter value in variable declaration: CSVWriter does not implement TagWriter (missing method WriteTag)
  FAIL  go-planttags [build failed]

The TagWriter interface as it stands now is the agreed v2 surface — the CLI and the checklist scripts are being written against WriteTag, so the interface itself must not move. The rendered output for every device is settled with the label vendor and the badge-roll reconciliation has to keep counting; planttags_test.go pins all of that and stays exactly as it is. Make `go test -race ./...` pass with the smallest sensible diff.
notes: Interface-satisfaction drift: the TagWriter method was renamed Format -> WriteTag but the two v1 implementors (TextWriter, CSVWriter) were not updated — 3 non-test error sites (registry map literal x3 minus badge, plus the SampleSheet slice literal) — and the registry stores BadgeWriter by value while its WriteTag has a pointer receiver (necessarily: it mutates the Printed counter; the test type-asserts *BadgeWriter and checks the count, so weakening to a value receiver fails). Chain-reveal, documented per 5C law: QuickTag calls TextWriter{}.Format on the concrete type, which compiles at baseline and breaks only once Format is renamed. Tests name WriteTag directly, so reverse-renaming the interface is pinned off.
